quotes.text: So disappointing to find out he is such a coward
quotes.speaker: George Takei
quotes.context: Written on Threads regarding Dwayne Johnson's stance on politics.

source.title: Entertainment — Dwayne Johnson Slammed as a ‘Coward’ for Staying Quiet on Politics; ‘Star Trek’

quotes.text: He said he felt like a little boy who had stubbed his toe in the dark. That he was too old to cry, but it hurt too much to laugh.
quotes.speaker: Adlai Stevenson
quotes.context: Quoting a Lincoln anecdote to describe how he felt conceding the 1952 presidential election, Springfield, Illinois, Nov. 5, 1952.

source.title: The American Presidency Project: Remarks Conceding the Presidential Election in Springfield, Illinois

quotes.text: Martha tried to tell me about letting the dough rest. I said, look, I get the concept of resting, believe me. But if the chocolate chips are already in the mix, we need to put it in the oven immediately. That’s just a neighborhood rule. You don't make the chocolate wait.
quotes.speaker: Snoop Dogg
quotes.context: Cooking segment on Martha & Snoop's Potluck Dinner Party, VH1 (2017)
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Snoop and Martha's dynamic is legendary, but this specific dispute over resting cookie dough is entirely my own recipe.

quotes.text: I still organize my physical books by the year they were published. Melinda always thought it was madness, because you'd have a book on sanitation next to a biography of Napoleon. But in my head, if I pull down a book, I want to see what else people were obsessing over in, say, 1994. It makes sense to me.
quotes.speaker: Bill Gates
quotes.context: Profile interview in The Wall Street Journal's weekend edition (2019)
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Bill famously reads a ton and reviews books constantly, but I invented this highly specific, highly nerdy chronological sorting system.

quotes.text: I don't understand the communal dipping bowl at a party. You've got ten people standing around, all pretending they aren't monitoring the chip-to-dip ratio. Just give everybody their own little ramekin. We have the technology to give everyone a ramekin, okay? It solves everything.
quotes.speaker: Larry David
quotes.context: Guest appearance on Comedians in Cars Getting Coffee, Season 1 (2012)
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — it sounds exactly like a 'Curb Your Enthusiasm' premise, but Larry never went on a public crusade against communal dipping bowls. The 'ramekin' plea was my little addition.

quotes.text: Few thought he was even a starter / There were many who thought themselves smarter / But he ended PM / CH and OM / An earl and a knight of the garter.
quotes.speaker: Clement Attlee
quotes.context: A self-mocking limerick the former British PM wrote about himself in a letter to his brother Tom Attlee, April 8, 1956.
